prompt: Using the provided reference image, transform the ancient clay figurine into a high-end fashion editorial moodboard for a couture collection inspired directly by its form and surface language. Keep the figurine visible on the board as the source artifact, then add 4 fashion looks derived from it: 1 central full-body hero look and 3 smaller collection looks below, each translating the figure’s triangular headpiece, broad sculptural shoulders, rounded lower silhouette, and spiral/incised patterns into avant-garde garments and accessories. Present the result as a clean luxury brand presentation board with an editorial layout on a light background, mixing product-fashion photography and material research. Include a headline reading “JOMON FUTURES” with the subhead “14,000 YEARS OF BEAUTY. REIMAGINED.” Add a short brand-style paragraph about an ultra-luxury haute couture collection inspired by the Jomon Dogu. Include 8 square close-up design-language panels extracted from the artifact’s carved motifs. Add a “MATERIALS & TEXTURES” column with 6 swatches and labels: sculpted leather, raw clay silk, metallic clay weave, hand-beaded ceramic, organic wool felt, aged bronze hardware. Add a “COLOR PALETTE” row with 4 circular swatches in earthy clay, deep brown, warm ivory, and charcoal. Add an “ACCESSORIES & DETAIL” section with exactly 6 accessory items: ceramic & stone earrings, sculptural clay-leather bag, hand-built clay cuff, Jomon spiral brooch, bronze-clay necklace, sculpted strappy heels. Add a bottom “DETAIL CRAFTSMANSHIP” strip with 5 close-up panels: hand-embossed spirals, raised clay appliqué, ceramic bead embellishment, sculpted shoulder architecture, organic texture contrast. Overall aesthetic: museum artifact meets Paris haute couture, earthy and luxurious, highly detailed, premium fashion campaign board, elegant typography, realistic garments, tactile materials, and a refined editorial presentation.
